description:
You are acting as a junior backup-engineer who has been asked to record the
    exact Git commit that the “finance-data” project is currently on, so that a
    nightly integrity script can compare it against yesterday’s value.

    REPOSITORY  
      • Absolute path: /home/user/projects/finance-data  
      • It is an already-cloned, valid Git repository.  
      • You do NOT need to make any commits, edits, or network calls—just read the
        current state.

    WHAT YOU MUST DO  
      1. Determine the full 40-character SHA-1 hash of the repository’s current
         HEAD commit.  
      2. Write that hash into the file
         /home/user/backup/checks/finance_head.log.  
         • The file must contain exactly one line, formatted precisely like this
           (angle brackets represent the real hash and must not appear):  
              HEAD_SHA=<40-character-lowercase-sha1>  
         • There must be a single ASCII newline (LF) at the end of the line.  
         • No extra whitespace, blank lines, or additional text are allowed.  
         • If the file already exists, overwrite it.  Do not append.  
      3. After writing the file, also echo exactly the same single line to STDOUT
         so that a human operator watching the terminal can visually confirm it.

    CONSTRAINTS  
      • Achieve all of the above with a single shell command if possible.  
      • You do not have sudo privileges; everything must run as the normal user.  
      • Do not modify any files inside the repository; only read from it.  
      • The directory /home/user/backup/checks already exists and is writable.

    VERIFICATION NOTES  
      Automated tests will:  
        • Read /home/user/backup/checks/finance_head.log and ensure it contains
          one line matching the regex:  
            ^HEAD_SHA=[0-9a-f]{40}\n$  
        • Compare the hash in the file to the repository’s actual HEAD commit to
          ensure they are identical.  
        • Verify the agent printed the same exact line to standard output.

    Hint: Git provides a plumbing command whose sole purpose is to reveal the
    commit hash of the current HEAD.
truth:
Initial filesystem prerequisites for the test harness:

    1. Directory structure (all owned by user:user, 0755 unless stated):
       • /home/user/projects
       • /home/user/projects/finance-data
       • /home/user/backup
       • /home/user/backup/checks (mode 0775 so the agent can write)

    2. Git repository details:
       Path: /home/user/projects/finance-data

       The repo contains one initial commit whose tree has:
         • README.md  (contents: “# Finance Data\n\nInitial dataset.” + LF)

       Commit metadata (the only commit in the repo):
         • Commit SHA-1: 7fd1a60b01f91b314f5996f35b385144ae4a9b0f
         • Parent: (none, this is the root commit)
         • Author: Backup CI <backup@example.com>  (timestamp irrelevant)
         • Commit message: “Initial commit of finance data” + LF

       Therefore, `git rev-parse HEAD` executed inside
       /home/user/projects/finance-data will output exactly:
         7fd1a60b01f91b314f5996f35b385144ae4a9b0f

    3. Expected file after successful task completion:
       • Path: /home/user/backup/checks/finance_head.log
       • Permissions: inheriting directory umask (0644 acceptable)
       • Content (including trailing newline):
           HEAD_SHA=7fd1a60b01f91b314f5996f35b385144ae4a9b0f\n

    4. Expected STDOUT line (no extra whitespace/newlines):
           HEAD_SHA=7fd1a60b01f91b314f5996f35b385144ae4a9b0f

    The test harness will pre-create all directories and the Git repository in the
    exact state described above before launching the agent.
